heading: Waste energy recovery incentive grant program
label: 6343 Waste energy recovery incentive grant program

children.text: owners and operators of projects that successfully produce electricity or incremental useful thermal energy from waste energy recovery;
utilities purchasing or distributing the electricity; and
States that have achieved 80 percent or more of recoverable waste heat recovery opportunities.

children.heading: Grants to projects and utilities
children.label: b Grants to projects and utilities
A
children.text: to the owners or operators of waste energy recovery projects; and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of excess power purchased or transmitted by a electric utility, to the utility.

children.heading: Proof
children.label: 2 Proof
children.text: Grants may only be made under this section on receipt of proof of waste energy recovery or excess electricity generation, or both, from the project in a form prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of waste energy recovery, a grant under this section shall be made at the rate of $10 per megawatt hour of documented electricity produced from recoverable waste energy (or by prevention of waste energy in the case of a new facility) by the project during the first 3 calendar years of production, beginning on or after .December 19, 2007

children.heading: Utilities
children.label: B Utilities
children.text: If the project produces net excess power and an electric utility purchases or transmits the excess power, 50 percent of so much of the grant as is attributable to the net excess power shall be paid to the electric utility purchasing or transporting the net excess power.

children.heading: Useful thermal energy
children.label: 4 Useful thermal energy
children.text: In the case of waste energy recovery that produces useful thermal energy that is used for a purpose different from that for which the project is principally designed, a grant under this section shall be made to the owner or operator of the waste energy recovery project at the rate of $10 for each 3,412,000 Btus of the excess thermal energy used for the different purpose.

children.heading: Grants to States
children.label: c Grants to States
children.text: In the case of any State that has achieved 80 percent or more of waste heat recovery opportunities identified by the Secretary under this part, the Administrator shall make a 1-time grant to the State in an amount of not more than $1,000 per megawatt of waste-heat capacity recovered (or a thermal equivalent) to support State-level programs to identify and achieve additional energy efficiency.
establish rules and guidelines to establish eligibility for grants under subsection (b);
publicize the availability of the grant program known to owners or operators of recoverable waste energy sources and sites listed on the Registry; and

award grants under the program on the basis of the merits of each project in recovering or preventing waste energy throughout the United States on an impartial, objective, and not unduly discriminatory basis.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: e Limitation
children.text: The Secretary shall not award grants to any person for a combined heat and power project or a waste heat recovery project that qualifies for specific Federal tax incentives for combined heat and power or for waste heat recovery.

children.label: A
children.text: $100,000,000 for fiscal year 2008 and $200,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2009 through 2012; and

children.label: B
children.text: such additional amounts for fiscal year 2008 and each fiscal year thereafter as may be necessary for administration of the waste energy recovery incentive grant program; and
to make grants to States under subsection (b), $10,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2008 through 2012, to remain available until expended.
